feedback.text: Looking to add a bit of feedback however small it might be but I would like to draw attention to at the time of this post the situation regarding games that were pulled off both Steam and Itch.io in late July 2025, both hosted games for sale by developers that one could consider controversial themes but which were otherwise legal. An Australian Group Collective Shout that supposedly campaigns against abuse tried getting certain games that they cite having themes of rape and incest taken down by steam and failed to do so. They then campaigned to Mastercard and Visa who shortly after reportedly forced Steam to take down and instigate changes to their rules that basically said games must comply with the terms of their payment processors and THIS is where the actual problem and unfairness begins. These 2 Payment processors combined have such a large control of the market that it could be considered an abuse of their position and anti competitive firstly and secondly that they were able to threaten steam and itch.io with suspension of their payment processing unless they complied meant that they were able to stop the sale of while controversial was still legal material and this is a major issue which could be expanded and exploited down the line to censor and stymie the sale of far more mainstream products. In fact another game called Mouthwashing which was not a pornographic game but a horror game was reported missing from itch.io at the time of writing this all over the fallout from this policy and if this could happen to them it could eventually affect other titles in the future such as Nier Automata, Stellar Blade, Grand Theft Auto, Call of Duty, Battlefield or Wuthering Waves as examples or more mainstream subjects like LGBT etc, simply because some people don't like such topics and this would result in such groups being able to enforce a policy of censorship by a private company outside the control and competence of EU or National governments. What I would call for in order to clamp down on such abuse is that in the interest of Digital Fairness that such payment processors are regulated that it be illegal to simply threaten yet alone, to withdraw payment services to companies like itch.io or steam for selling perfectly legal products (even if controversial) and only are legally allowed to do so should said products be explicitly ruled unfit for sale by either a national government, court order or a state authority that has competence in such an area. This is something that should be done not only to ensure market fairness but to prevent unjustified censorship by stealth, for a private entity to be able to abuse their position to influence the market and to prevent foreign groups being intentionally or unintentionally able to influence business by proxy. I don't know if my feedback will be read or considered but I do hope if someone does read this that they're able to appreciate my concerns on these issues that I've raised and that it helps in shaping such acts to prevent future abuse.
